content.problemStatement:
Given an integer N, you can apply a operation on NNN multiple times(possibly 0).

Operation is defined as follows:


Choose a positive integer KKK such that KKK has exactly 444 divisors and KKK is a divisor of NNN.
Change NNN to N/KN/KN/K.
Find the minimum number of divisors possible of the resulting number NNN.

Input:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains an integers NNN.
Output:For each test case, output a single line containing one integer — the minimum number of divisors possible of the resulting number.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We cannot divide N=3N=3N=3 by any valid KKK and hence output is 222 as N=3N=3N=3 has exactly two divisors 111 and 333.
Example case 2: N=64N=64N=64 can be divided by 888 twice as 888 has exactly 444 divisors ( 111, 222, 444, 888 )  and hence the resultant value of NNN will be 111 and 111 has only 111 divisor hence answer is 111.
Example case 3: We can divide N=30N=30N=30 by 666, 101010 or 151515 as all three of them have exactly 444 divisors. In each case resultant value of NNN will be either 555, 333 or 222. And all three of them has exactly 222 divisors and they cannot be divided further. Hence the output is 222.
Example case 4: One of the optimal ways to divide N=120N=120N=120 is divide 120120120 by 888 followed by dividing  by 151515. The resultant value will be 120/8=15120/8 = 15120/8=15 and 15/15=115/15 = 115/15=1. The number 111 has exactly one divisor and hence output is 111.
Note that, If we divided N=120N=120N=120 by 666 then we won't get the optimal answer.
Example case 5: We can divide N=45N=45N=45 by 151515 and resultant value of NNN will be 333. The number 333 has exactly 222 divisors and hence output is 222
